metadata.title:
HOBOES

sUPERINTENDEN BEAMER Is
SUES ORDERS TO TRAINMEN
TO PREVENT KNIGHTS OF THE
ROAD TRAVELLING BY THE BOX
CAR, BLIND BAGGAGE AND
BRAKEBEAM RoUTEs.

pages.raw_text:
The cheerful and careless hobo has
already started on his semi-annual
migratons and to date several of them
have rushed the season and started
to migrate before the warm rays of
the sun penetrate their hides. Super
intendent Beamer is also out early with
instructions as to how to catch the hobo
mapping and stop his free use of the
box car for his Pullman sleeper and
day coach. In orders issued to train
men today he says that whenever any
hoboes take to travelling via the freight
line they must be ordered off the
train and put off where force is nee
essary. WWherever tramps are found
on train, their number, their des-
tination and where taken on must be
reported to headquarters.


Trainmen Eave Harvest.


During the summer season trainmen
have a rich harvest from the money
collected from tramps who load them
selves onto the trains. Many crews
leave convenient freight car unlock
ed This is used as trap for Mr.
Tramp who stealthily climbs into the
car and curls up in one corner on a
sheet of newspaper or bandanna hand
kerchief. This is done not exactly FOL
cleanliness but as a menss of protection
from the dust which is an unmistak
able sign to a policeman that the man
has come over the hobo route, and re-
suits in his being ordered from the
city or 70 the chain gang.


Not all who travel by the box cal
line are tramps. Many hard-workinl
men who do not care to pay three cents
a mile had rather put up with the dis
comorts of a hard-foored box car, with
their arm for a. pillow, and pay the
brakeman at the rate of one cent &
mile OF even a half cent.
